columns.name: dryer_speed_bias
columns.description: Operator-adjusted 0-10 bias on drying section line speed, where higher values push the sheet through faster.

columns.name: wet_end_drain_index
columns.description: Inline wet-end sensor index that noisily reflects how freely water leaves the fiber web before the dryers.

columns.name: roll_quality_score
columns.description: Final composite quality score for the finished paper roll based on moisture uniformity, strength, and surface finish.

stakes.narrative: The mill team believes increasing dryer speed bias improves throughput without hurting the finished roll and may even help quality by stabilizing the sheet path. But historical settings were chosen during runs when incoming pulp drainability was changing, and when drainability was high operators tended to turn the speed bias down because the sheet already seemed easy to dry. Since that same latent condition also improves final roll quality, the observational records can make the knob look less useful than it really is. Controlled experiments that set the bias directly are therefore more informative than routine production history.

functionals.name: exceedance
functionals.brief_clause: a scrap -- roll_quality_score below the acceptance line of 24.7 -- is far costlier than a forgone gain

control_surface.deliverable_note: model(regime, n, seed) -> table over [dryer_speed_bias, wet_end_drain_index, roll_quality_score].
